findings.evidence: New lines 65-84 are exactly `simpa [add_halves] using hF.diagonal_* (t / 2)`; existing API is at `TauCeti/Analysis/PositiveDefinite/SemigroupGroup.lean:262` `diagonal_nonneg`, `:268` `diagonal_im`, `:273` `diagonal_re_nonneg`, and `:279` `diagonal_eq_ofReal_re`.
findings.fix: Delete these wrappers and use `hF.diagonal_nonneg (t / 2)`, `hF.diagonal_im (t / 2)`, `hF.diagonal_re_nonneg (t / 2)`, and `hF.diagonal_eq_ofReal_re (t / 2)` with `simpa [add_halves]`.
findings.issue: `timeSlice_apply_zero_nonneg`, `timeSlice_apply_zero_im`, `timeSlice_apply_zero_re_nonneg`, and `timeSlice_apply_zero_eq_ofReal_re` are just the existing diagonal lemmas specialized at `t / 2`.

summary: The fixed-time kernel reduction is new, but the origin normal-form lemmas duplicate the existing semigroup-group diagonal API after substituting `t / 2`. Remove those wrappers or justify a separate API boundary for them.
